profile.name: Gabriel Ong
profile.role: Product-minded Software Developer specialising in Legal Technology
profile.intro: I like making things. I've been writing software for 6 years and thinking about product for 1.

works.title: hot cross buns
works.summary: FOSS Google Calendar & Tasks Wrapper for MacOS
detail: This was my first real user-facing project where I thoroughly iterated on both the systems (backend, fault tolerance, optimization) and product (UX, frontend, user journey).

My main takeaway from building it out was that the software creation really only took up about 35% of the initial effort, and validation + user feedback played a much bigger role in helping me understand how actual people were using Hot Cross Buns.

works.title: goodman
detail: Quite literally threw this together over a weekend because I was getting so frustrated with the absurd number of websites that intentionally obscure their terms and conditions pages behind auto-approvals and nested subpages.
GitHub repo here https://github.com/gongahkia/goodman if you're interested.

works.title: sea kayak
works.summary: Singapore's Daily Legal Updates Web App
detail: Made this with the hope that it'd encourage a more consistent approach towards consuming bite-sized legal updates in Singapore
This project also kickstarted me beginning to put in effort into prettifying the frontends of my projects.
I think I'm the only user at the moment, but check it out here at https://sea-kayak.vercel.app/ if it sounds like something you'd enjoy.

works.title: junas
detail: Building Junas forced me to hit the ground running with agent harnesses and guardrails, but more importantly taught me how to scope (and cut) product features based on real user impact and needs.

Implementing more features just because the engineering is fun and the tech is cool is tempting, but if it leads to building a solution that's looking for a problem, then its likely you're building something noone would actually use.
The project is here at https://github.com/gongahkia/junas for those interested. I might continue iterating on it in the future.

works.title: skill hunter
detail: My first substantial project (built for people other than just myself) that also catapulted me into the wonderful world of browser extension scripting.

More significantly, building and deploying Skill Hunter showed me that more often than not, the products with the highest adoption metrics are just small tools that solve a specific problem well with minimal installation friction.
Not every software problem has to be solved with a full-blown app. If reading SSO Legislation sometimes feels overwhelming, consider checking it out at https://github.com/gongahkia/skill-hunter.

works.title: yuho
works.summary: Domain-specific language for Singapore Criminal Law
detail: I've always been interested in the notion of rules-as-code in systems design, and this idea of creating a DSL that could encode Singapore statutes just so happened to come at a time when I was also actively learning about handwriting interpreters (and to a lesser extent, esolangs).
So Yuho was basically the convergence of all these factors interesecting at the perfect time.

It's not much of a product in the traditional sense (if anything its closer to a proof-of-concept), but it's one of my proudest and most thoroughly-researched pet projects by far. You can find it here at https://github.com/gongahkia/yuho.
(Edit on 1 May 2026: I have since written and published a paper about Yuho on Zenodo hre https://zenodo.org/records/19935537. Take a look if you like reading about language frameworks!)
